mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show a positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses the 5.0 percent threshold, labor market slack accelerates, signaling deteriorating corporate revenue growth and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 70 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth by more than 2 percentage points, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during post-shock recovery phases reflects rapid capacity utilization and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat forward returns during recovery phases, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days of signal generation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly expand aggregate wage income and consumer spending capacity, mechanically supporting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical relationship with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.4 percent forces central banks to accelerate rate hikes, raising borrowing costs and compressing equity multiples, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in response to CPI spikes above 0.4 percent, or the negative predictive relationship reverses during high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding real GDP signals synchronized strength in residential investment and broad economic output, confirming durable demand growth that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal generates negative forward returns when HOUST growth outpaces GDPC1 growth by more than 3 percentage points, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month acceleration in real personal consumption expenditures during early-cycle recovery phases indicates immediate consumer confidence restoration, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: RSAFS acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early recovery phases, or the signal directional accuracy drops below 52 percent in out-of-sample classification tests.
